summary: Push Notifications Testing - Backend APIs all passing (12/12), Frontend NotificationBell component functional with design consideration. Backend correctly stores/fetches notifications, registers FCM tokens, and marks notifications as read. Frontend NotificationBell displays dropdown with 'Enable Notifications' prompt when browser permission is not granted - this blocks notification list view until permission is granted (design decision).

issues: When browser notification permission is 'denied' or 'default', the dropdown shows 'Enable Notifications' prompt instead of the notification list. This blocks users from viewing notifications fetched from backend unless they grant notification permission. Consider showing notifications list even when push permission is denied, with a smaller 'enable push' prompt.
Banner returns null when permission='denied', so users who denied permission don't see the prompt - this is expected behavior but worth noting

passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/notifications/register-token - registers FCM token successfully
Backend: POST /api/notifications/register-token - updates existing token
Backend: POST /api/notifications/register-token - validates required fields (422 for missing fields)
Backend: POST /api/notifications/send - sends notification and stores in DB
Backend: POST /api/notifications/send - supports all notification types (message, payment, document, property, booking, review, maintenance, system)
Backend: POST /api/notifications/send - validates required fields
Backend: GET /api/notifications/{user_id} - fetches all notifications
Backend: GET /api/notifications/{user_id}?unread_only=true - filters for unread
Backend: GET /api/notifications/{user_id} - returns empty array for new users
Backend: POST /api/notifications/mark-read/{notification_id} - marks notification as read
Backend: POST /api/notifications/mark-read/{notification_id} - handles non-existent ID gracefully
Backend: Full notification flow - register token → send → fetch → mark read → verify
Frontend: NotificationBell component displays in dashboard header
Frontend: NotificationBell shows unread indicator (red dot) when hasUnread=true
Frontend: NotificationBell dropdown opens on click
Frontend: NotificationBell dropdown shows 'Notifications' header
Frontend: NotificationBell dropdown shows 'Mark all read' button
Frontend: NotificationBell dropdown shows 'Refresh' button
Frontend: NotificationBell shows 'Enable Notifications' button when permission not granted
Frontend: NotificationBell shows 'Stay Updated' prompt for users without push permission
action_items: OPTIONAL: Consider modifying NotificationBell to show notification list even when browser notification permission is denied, with a smaller 'enable push notifications' banner above the list instead of blocking the entire notification view
critical_code_review_comments: NotificationBell.jsx properly uses data-testid attributes for all interactive elements
NotificationBell correctly fetches notifications from backend on mount regardless of permission status
NotificationPrompt correctly handles all permission states (supported, not-supported, granted, denied, default, dismissed)
Backend notification endpoints properly handle FCM token registration and notification persistence
All components use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL for API calls correctly

success_rate.frontend: 90% - All components working, design consideration noted

seed_data_creation: Sent test notifications via API for testing (message, payment, property types)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 15 tested Push Notifications feature. Backend APIs fully working: /api/notifications/register-token, /api/notifications/send, /api/notifications/{user_id}, /api/notifications/mark-read/{id}. Frontend NotificationBell component works but shows 'Enable Notifications' prompt when browser permission is not granted (blocking notification list view). This is a design decision - notifications are fetched from backend but not displayed unless permission=granted. NotificationPrompt banner correctly hides when permission is 'denied'. Test user has 19 notifications in DB.

Backend_Notification_APIs.notes: All endpoints working correctly with proper validation and error handling

NotificationBell_Component.notes: Shows 'Enable Notifications' prompt when permission not granted - blocks notification list view (design decision)

NotificationPrompt_Banner.notes: Returns null when permission='denied' - expected behavior

FCM_Token_Registration.notes: Tokens registered correctly when available, fcm_token_available returned in send response
